title: anemone pratensis subsp. hungarica
intro: Anemone pratensis subsp. hungarica, also known as the Hungarian anemone, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This perennial plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving rheumatic pain, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the roots and rhizomes of the Hungarian anemone are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
